reasoning: South Sudan's AfCFTA ratification bill has been pending in the National Assembly since July 2021 (4+ years of blockage). As of January 2025, South Sudan remains one of only 7 African countries that haven't ratified. Ratification progress continent-wide has been extremely slow (only 47→48 countries between late 2023 and April 2025). South Sudan's government is focused on peace talks, sanctions renewal, and political transition issues. The 2-month window to July 1, 2025 is too short to overcome institutional inertia. Strong negative evidence from UNDP (search_3_summary) and January 2025 status reports (search_0_result_3, search_1_result_6) outweighs any diplomatic pressure. Probability set at 12% to account for small chance of sudden political breakthrough.
